reread: both candidates describe TokenServiceImpl.getToken(BasicAuthDto,HttpHeaders), the token-issuance method; predicate classification (handlesCommand vs servesQuery) differs but same underlying method

reference records that TokenServiceImpl.getToken returns a TokenDto carrying userId/username/jwt on the success branch; neither candidate packet carries an atom describing the TokenDto return-shape, only the status-envelope semantics

reference records an ordering precondition (verifyCode must verify true before username/password authentication proceeds); no candidate atom states this ordering requirement, only the resulting status-envelope branch

reference records a single compound ensures-clause (status set to cancel, refund computed, drawback issued together on the success branch); each constituent fact exists as a separate candidate atom already placed under its own reference row (state transition, drawback effect, response status), but no candidate atom states the compound guarantee as one contract

reread: CancelServiceImpl.java calculateRefund returns zero refund when order status is NOTPAID or when travel start time has passed, otherwise price*0.8 via DecimalFormat (lines ~162,200,224,230); candidates capture this rule as semantic statusGates/meansInUse atoms rather than a contract ensures atom

reread: CancelServiceImpl.java forwards the Authorization header to downstream order/user/payment/notification calls only when present (lines 254,257); candidate captured it as an authority atom rather than a contract requires atom
reread: both candidates describe the same NOTPAID/PAID/CHANGE eligibility gate for cancellation in CancelServiceImpl.java (lines 52,109), captured as semantic meansInUse rather than contract requires

reread: SwaggerConfig.java selects api-docs by basePackage from the per-service swagger.controllerPackage property (lines 21,31); candidates capture the same fact under contract/shapedAs and semantic/meansInUse respectively
reread: PUT ts-order-service /api/v1/orderservice/order at CancelServiceImpl.java:245 persists the order status change
reread: PUT ts-order-other-service /api/v1/orderOtherService/orderOther at CancelServiceImpl.java:269

reread: GET ts-inside-payment-service drawback endpoint credits refund amount at CancelServiceImpl.java:284; sibling run-2 candidate for the same target endpoint is placed under the parallel relation:calls-insidepaymentservice row to avoid reusing the same candidate id twice

reread: sendEmail() defines the POST ts-notification-service order_cancel_success call (CancelServiceImpl.java:143-149); its only call site in cancelOrder is a commented-out line (line 87, confirmed against source: '// sendEmail(notifyInfo, headers);'); sibling run-2 candidate for the same target is placed under the parallel relation:calls-notificationservice row
run-2 notes record AuthenticationManager.authenticate as observed but deliberately not written as an atom; no candidate atom (relation or otherwise) covers this call
